workflows.name: Routing
workflows.tagline: The intake lane: every request enters here so the Strategist can pick the right real workflow.
workflows.when: Use this as the universal front door for any new request — quick or large. The Butler opens Routing on every intake turn; the Strategist then selects the real workflow (Quick Fix, Standard Mission, Architecture Build, Design Sprint, etc.) and the turn continues under that banner. Routing itself does no specialist or doer work — it routes.

steps.title: Submit the Request
steps.act: You describe what you want the guild to do, in plain language.

steps.title: Restate the Brief
steps.act: The Butler (the session voice) restates the request as a one-line brief and halts for the Guild Master's explicit approval — the approval gate.

steps.title: Pick the Real Workflow
steps.act: The Strategist selects the actual workflows.json entry that fits (Quick Fix · Standard Mission · Architecture Build · Design Sprint · Legal Codex · Market Recon · Skill Forge · …) — or opens Workflow Forge if none fits. The Strategist chooses; the Butler does not.

steps.label: The Butler reports the chosen workflow to the Guild Master in plain English and asks for go — the actual specialist work begins under that workflow's banner.

workflows.name: Standard Mission
workflows.tagline: The full guild pipeline for serious, multi-step missions.
workflows.when: Use this when the work is large, multi-disciplinary, or needs several specialists coordinated in waves — the guild's conquering-campaign, planned and driven to completion.

steps.title: Place the Order
steps.act: You describe the mission in plain language so the guild knows what to build.

steps.label: You review and approve the brief before the guild begins planning.

steps.title: Form the Campaign Plan
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Design the Interface
steps.act: The Designer shapes the visual design and UI for any user-facing part of the mission, so the build has a clear visual target to hit. The guild's only designer — no one else touches the look.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the plan and design are buildable, complete, and safe to start construction.

steps.title: Implement the Plan
steps.act: The Developer writes the code, fixes any bugs, and self-tests everything before handing it back.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs final cleanup checks and confirms the whole mission works end to end.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the finished mission — what was built and any follow-ups — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Quick Fix
workflows.tagline: A lean path for small, well-understood changes.
workflows.when: Use this for small bugs, UI tweaks, or a pasted error to fix (and any of the same class) where planning would only slow things down.

steps.title: Report the Bug
steps.act: You flag a small, well-understood bug or change for the guild to fix.

steps.label: You confirm the fix scope is correct before the Developer starts work.

steps.title: Make the Fix
steps.act: The Developer applies the smallest correct change and tests it locally before handing it off.

steps.title: Verify the Fix
steps.act: The Quartermaster double-checks the change and confirms nothing else broke in the process.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the fix and its verification — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Design Sprint
workflows.tagline: From visual concept to shipped UI in one tight sprint.
workflows.when: Use this when the work centers on UI, UX, brand, or visual quality — including admin/members panel layout redesigns and card/panel consolidation, the most frequent visual work on the app.

steps.title: Request the Sprint
steps.act: You describe the UI or UX problem you want the guild to design around.

steps.label: You approve the design brief before the sprint officially kicks off.

steps.title: Plan the Sprint
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Design the Visuals
steps.act: The Designer produces the UI/UX mockups, components, and brand treatments for the sprint.

steps.title: Build the Design
steps.act: The Developer implements the design faithfully in code, respecting the visual spec.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster polishes the build and verifies the design holds up in real interactions.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the design-sprint outcome, with screenshots and notes — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Architecture Build
workflows.tagline: Schemas, systems, and structural change, done right.
workflows.when: Use this when the work touches the database, system design, or core architecture — including performance tuning and finance-critical data migrations (plan, dry-run with BEGIN/ROLLBACK, reconcile).

steps.title: Request the Build
steps.act: You describe the system, schema, or architectural change you need the guild to make.

steps.label: You approve the architecture brief before scoping and structural work begin.

steps.title: Scope the Work
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Architect the System
steps.act: The Architect drafts the system architecture and database schema to satisfy the scope.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the architecture is sound, consistent, and safe to implement.

steps.title: Build Migrations and Code
steps.act: The Developer writes the schema migrations and implements the system against the certified design.

steps.title: Integrate the Modules
steps.act: The Developer assembles the slice diffs, reconciles seams against the shared contract, runs the build, and relies on the inline verify-gate Critic to review the aggregate diff before a single commit.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs final checks and confirms the architecture works as intended in production-like conditions.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the architecture build, its verification, and any caveats — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Legal Codex
workflows.tagline: Turn real laws into a clean, multilingual codex.
workflows.when: Use this to load real laws into the codex and translate them across locales — loop the source laws, load + translate each, and stop at the publish gate (you publish). Fed by Law Harvest — run that first to build the verified source-law library.

steps.title: Submit the Law
steps.act: You point the guild at the real-world law or regulation you need codified.

steps.label: You confirm the scope of the codex work and the target locales before translation begins.

steps.title: Load and Translate
steps.act: The Translator ingests the real law and translates it faithfully across each required locale.

steps.title: Run Publish Check
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs the pre-publish check on the codex and signs off on its readiness.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the codex summary — locales covered and publication status — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Market Recon
workflows.tagline: Read-only trading, investment, and market intelligence.
workflows.when: Use this for investment, trading, or market research where no code should be written. (For website/SEO/brand audits use Brand Audit.)

steps.title: Ask the Question
steps.act: You pose a research or investment question you want the guild to analyze.

steps.label: You approve the research brief and confirm the read-only boundary before analysis begins.

steps.title: Analyze Market and Risk
steps.act: The Merchant investigates the market, prices, and risk in a read-only analysis pass and writes a report.

steps.label: The Butler reports the Merchant's market and risk report, with caveats — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Skill Forge
workflows.tagline: Keep the guild's skills sharp, synced, and current.
workflows.when: Use this for guild self-maintenance — skill sync, upgrades, creating a new skill, or turning a one-off task into a reusable skill.

steps.title: Request Skill Maintenance
steps.act: You ask the guild to sync, upgrade, or create skills for the team.

steps.title: Sync and Upgrade Skills
steps.act: The Quartermaster syncs, upgrades, or authors the logic and content of the skills that were requested.

steps.title: Forge the Sigil
steps.act: MANDATORY for every new skill: the Designer forges its Fallen Sword tile so it ships with real heraldry, never a bare emoji. Add the skill's prompt to gen-skill-art.cjs and run it (or guild/commission_art.py from a brief), then build.py so artPng flips true. Skill art is design — it runs through the Designer, no one else.

steps.title: Provide Tooling Support
steps.act: The Developer provides any tooling, dev-environment, or knowledge-graph support the skill changes require.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the skill-forge outcome — version changes and tooling status — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Guild Log Sync
workflows.tagline: Sweep recent sessions and backfill any missing guild-log entries.
workflows.when: Use this to reconcile the guild log after a stretch of work — loop the recent sessions, find changes that never got recorded, and log them. First stage of the release-closeout chain: Guild Log Sync → Hygiene Rotation → Release Train.

steps.title: Call the Log Sweep
steps.act: You ask the guild to loop the recent sessions (today and yesterday) and check the guild log for any change that never got recorded.

steps.label: You confirm the Butler understood the order correctly before the sweep begins.
The Quartermaster certifies each candidate entry is real, non-duplicate, and correctly typed before anything is written to the log.

steps.title: Log the Missing Entries
steps.act: The Quartermaster, drawing MiniMax M3, runs the two-tier pipeline — build_guild_log.py to backfill git-visible commits, log_event.py for the non-git events — then build.py to regenerate the dashboard data.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was logged — git-tier catch-up plus manual entries — and the new log total — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Compliance Audit
workflows.tagline: The Quartermaster runs the full compliance pass — OKF frontmatter tidy then guild conformity check — leaving the repo agreeing with itself and every logged decision.
workflows.when: Use after big changes, when frontmatter drifts, or any time you need proof the project is OKF-conformant and internally consistent. Replaces running Conformity Sweep and OKF Tidy separately. Pass mode=okf or mode=conformity to run only one pass.

steps.title: Call the Compliance Audit
steps.act: You ask the guild to run the full compliance pass — OKF tidy and/or guild conformity — or name a specific mode (okf | conformity | both, default both).

steps.label: You confirm the scope before any file is rewritten — a whole-repo frontmatter migration is hard to eyeball after the fact.

steps.title: OKF Audit (skip if mode=conformity)
steps.act: Runs  to report every governed .md lacking YAML frontmatter or a  field, and surveys non-markdown files for orphans and misplacement.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the OKF migration is safe — exclusions cover vendored/generated trees and build.py still parses members & skills after frontmatter is injected.

steps.title: OKF Migrate & Sweep (skip if mode=conformity)
steps.act: Runs , enriches concepts with title/description/tags and cross-links (handing bulk drafting to a doer weapon), places non-md files by concept-path, prunes confirmed dead files, then runs build.py. Re-runs until it passes clean. Confirms okf-gate hook is registered in .claude/settings.json.

steps.title: Conformity Audit (skip if mode=okf)
steps.act: Drawing MiniMax M3, runs conformity_check.py — reading every source (members, skills, arsenal, workflows, docs, guild log) plus generated guild data, cross-checking every reference and testing each against logged decisions — to assemble a contradiction map.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies each flagged contradiction is real and not a false positive before any source is touched.

steps.title: Reconcile & Rebuild (skip if mode=okf)
steps.act: Fixes each real contradiction at its source of truth, runs build.py so the generated guild data matches the sources again, re-runs conformity_check.py until it passes clean.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs  — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown. Runs guild-log to record the pass.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was audited, migrated, reconciled, and gated — OKF status and conformity status both, with a flag for whether the okf-gate is now armed.

workflows.name: Bug Cycle
workflows.tagline: Pull a reported bug, hunt the real root cause, fix it, and close the loop.
workflows.when: Use this for bugs filed in the bug_reports table — the daily triage-and-fix loop. The recurring Lex signatures that belong here: a missing i18n message key rendering raw in the UI, a missing DB column, or an RLS / permission-denied error on a view or RPC.

steps.title: Report or Pull the Bug
steps.act: You file a bug, or ask the guild to pull the open rows from the bug_reports table.

steps.label: You confirm the bug and its scope before the hunt begins.

steps.title: Triage & Reproduce
steps.act: The Strategist pulls the row, reads primary_instructions, and reproduces the report to find the true root cause — not just the symptom.

steps.title: Fix at the Root
steps.act: The Developer applies the smallest correct fix following project conventions, and self-tests it locally.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the fix genuinely resolves the bug — DB fixes count now; frontend fixes only after deploy — before the status is flipped.

steps.title: Close & File Spawn
steps.act: The Developer flips the bug_reports row to Fixed and files any new bugs surfaced during the hunt back into the table.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the bug, its root cause, the fix, and any new bugs filed — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Security Sweep
workflows.tagline: Audit the database security surface and seal every leak — propose first, apply on sign-off.
workflows.when: Use this for RLS policies, SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, anon/authenticated grants, and advisor-driven security audits.

steps.title: Call the Sweep
steps.act: You ask the guild to audit the app's security surface for leaks and over-broad access.

steps.label: You confirm the read-only boundary before the audit begins.

steps.title: Scope the Surface
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Hunt the Holes
steps.act: The Architect audits the security surface across the OWASP Top-10 lens: pulls Supabase advisors and maps every leak, missing RLS policy, and over-broad grant (A01); scans for hardcoded secrets/credentials (A02), injection and unvalidated input (A03), and outdated/vulnerable dependencies via npm audit (A06).

steps.label: You sign off on each proposed change before any security fix is applied — nothing is written without explicit approval.

steps.title: Seal the Breaches
steps.act: The Developer applies the approved RLS/policy/grant fixes and re-proves each gate holds.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was audited, what was sealed, and any risks deferred — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Hygiene Rotation
workflows.tagline: The Quartermaster runs the full hygiene sweep across every mode.
workflows.when: Use this for routine hygiene or post-campaign cleanup — i18n, lint, dev-errors, postgres, docs, follow-ups, and consolidating duplicated search/filter/action UI across pages and panels. Middle stage of the release-closeout chain: Guild Log Sync → Hygiene Rotation → Release Train.

steps.title: Call the Rotation
steps.act: You ask the guild to run a hygiene pass — or the post-campaign cleanup for a just-shipped build.

steps.label: You confirm the scope of the sweep before it runs.

steps.title: Run the Sweep
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs the cleanup skill across every mode — i18n language/hardcoded/leaks, dev-error sweep, lint, postgres advisors, docs, and campaign follow-ups — fixing what's safe and listing the rest.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was swept, what was fixed, and what remains as follow-ups — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Sync Rotation
workflows.tagline: The Quartermaster proves the device still matches the repo, then reconciles any drift.
workflows.when: Use to answer 'is the device current / what else needs syncing' or after any skill/scheduled-task change. Sweeps every repo-to-device parity surface (skills, scheduled-tasks, members, config), reconciles repo-ahead skills via skillsmith, and closes with the conformity gate.

steps.title: Call the Sync
steps.act: You ask the guild whether the device still matches the repo — or to reconcile what has drifted.

steps.label: You confirm the scope — check-only or reconcile — before anything touches the device.

steps.title: Sweep the Device
steps.act: Runs `guild/device_sync.py` — sweep every repo-to-device parity surface and report drift.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports which surfaces matched, what was reconciled, and what drift remains — back to you in plain English.

trigger_phrases: is the device up to date

workflows.name: Release Train
workflows.tagline: Merge every branch and PR, bump the version, and push the release.
workflows.when: Use this to close out work — merge all branches/PRs into main, bump the version, and push. Final stage of the release-closeout chain: Guild Log Sync → Hygiene Rotation → Release Train.

steps.title: Call the Release
steps.act: You ask the guild to clean up, merge everything outstanding, bump the version, and push.

steps.label: You confirm the version step and that all branches are safe to merge before the train leaves.

steps.title: Gather the Branches
steps.act: The Quartermaster merges every outstanding branch and PR into main, resolving conflicts.

steps.title: Bump & Stamp
steps.act: The Quartermaster bumps the version, writes the changelog, and syncs every version stamp across the docs.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the build is clean and every version stamp is consistent before the push.

steps.title: Push the Release
steps.act: The Quartermaster commits and pushes the release to the remote.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the version shipped, what merged, and the push result — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Tool Forge
workflows.tagline: Build a public legal calculator or corporate tool from the governing law.
workflows.when: Use this to build a public-panel calculator or legal tool (wages, social-insurance, severance, inheritance, customs, land).

steps.title: Request the Tool
steps.act: You describe the legal calculator or tool you want to offer on the public panel.

steps.label: You approve the tool concept and its governing law before the build begins.

steps.title: Model the Law
steps.act: The Architect studies the governing law and models the calculation rules and inputs the tool must honour.

steps.title: Design the Tool
steps.act: The Designer shapes the calculator's UI with an inviting, trustworthy layout.

steps.title: Build the Calculator
steps.act: The Developer implements the tool, wires the rules, and adds the team-review disclaimer.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the tool built, the law it honours, and where it lives — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Legal Drafting
workflows.tagline: Draft client correspondence and bilingual legal documents in the firm's register.
workflows.when: Use this to draft client emails, contracts, declarations, or memos — bilingual (AR/FR/EN) where needed.

steps.title: Submit the Matter
steps.act: You describe the correspondence or legal instrument you need drafted.

steps.label: You confirm the matter and the target language(s) before drafting begins.

steps.title: Draft the Document
steps.act: The Translator drafts the correspondence or legal instrument — bilingual where needed — in the firm's register.

steps.label: You review the draft for accuracy and tone before it goes out — nothing client-facing is sent without your sign-off.

steps.title: Finalize & Format
steps.act: The Translator finalizes the document and formats it client-facing, with the bilingual layout in place.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the document drafted, its languages, and what's ready to send — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Comms Triage
workflows.tagline: Sweep email, calendar, and WhatsApp into tasks, events, and replies.
workflows.when: Use this to triage your inbox and messages — turn what matters into tasks and calendar entries, and prep for meetings.

steps.title: Call the Triage
steps.act: You ask the guild to go through your email, calendar, and WhatsApp and surface what needs you.

steps.label: You confirm the scope before the Butler drafts anything or creates events.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what needs you, what was scheduled, and the drafts awaiting your send — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Standing Watch
workflows.tagline: Define and run an unattended scheduled task on a cron cadence.
workflows.when: Use this for recurring automations that run unattended on a cron — housekeeping (daily/hourly/weekly), the daily Gmail/WhatsApp reports and reply-drafts, per-matter deadline reminders, colleague-progress nudges, the Supabase advisor, auto-improvement/auto-strategizing, and the law scanners. ~23 such tasks live under ~/Documents/Claude/Scheduled/; this is their shape.

steps.title: Define the Routine
steps.act: You describe the recurring job you want to run on a schedule, unattended.

steps.label: You approve the cadence and the unattended scope before the watch is set.

steps.title: Define the Watch
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Stand the Watch
steps.act: On each tick the routine runs unattended via its assigned weapons, does the work, and ends with a plain-English summary.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports what the routine does, its cadence, and the last run's summary — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Brand Audit
workflows.tagline: Audit the public site and brand from a marketing-firm perspective.
workflows.when: Use this for website/SEO/brand audits and campaign-readiness — the public panel, messaging, reach, and conversion. Led by the Herald, the guild's marketing member.

steps.title: Call the Recon
steps.act: You ask the guild to audit the public panel and brand as a marketing firm would.

steps.label: You confirm the audit lens before the recon begins.

steps.title: Frame the Lens
steps.act: The Herald frames the audit from a marketing-firm perspective — SEO, search-catchability, messaging truth, reach, and the conversion path.

steps.title: Audit Reach & Message
steps.act: The Herald audits the public site's reach, messaging, and conversion, flagging anything off-brand, untrue, or that weakens demand.

steps.title: Polish the Public Panel
steps.act: The Designer reviews the public site's visual polish and on-brand look — footer, hero, team, pricing — and fixes what's off.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the findings, the off-brand/untrue flags, and the campaign-readiness call — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Art Forge
workflows.tagline: Forge Fallen Sword art — sigils, portraits, weapon and workflow tiles — via MiniMax, gated by the Designer.
workflows.when: Use this whenever a new visual asset is needed — a member portrait, skill sigil, weapon tile, or workflow sigil.

steps.title: Request the Art
steps.act: You describe the art you need — what it's for, the subject, and any constraints.

steps.label: You approve the art brief before the forge is lit.

steps.title: Draft the Brief
steps.act: The Designer sets the art brief — subject, size, theme, palette, and what to avoid — in the Fallen Sword style.

steps.title: Forge via MiniMax
steps.act: The Designer commissions the image from the MiniMax doer-weapon, reviews each result, and sends rejects back with deltas until they land.

steps.title: Wire the Art
steps.act: The Developer drops the finals into the right path and rebuilds the dashboard so the art renders.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the art forged, where it lives, and how it renders — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Arsenal Forge
workflows.tagline: Recruit or re-skin a weapon — art, thinker/doer role, and wiring into every loadout.
workflows.when: Use this when a new AI model joins the arsenal, or an existing weapon needs re-skinning or re-roleing.

steps.title: Recruit the Weapon
steps.act: You name the model to add or change, and how it should look or behave.

steps.label: You approve the weapon's identity and role before it's forged and wired.

steps.title: Name the Weapon
steps.act: The Strategist sets the weapon's identity — model, element, colour, visual metaphor — and its role: thinker, doer, or both.

steps.title: Forge the Weapon Art
steps.act: The Designer commissions the weapon art via MiniMax and reviews it (runs Art Forge).

steps.title: Wire to the Arsenal
steps.act: The Quartermaster wires the weapon and its role-icon into the arsenal and every member's loadout, in priority order.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the weapon added, its role, and the loadouts updated — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Strategic Audit
workflows.tagline: Convene the multi-model panel, converge the takes, and ship a graded MD audit.
workflows.when: Use this when you want a deep judgement that ships a document, not code — repo audits, skill-merge analysis, 'does this achieve its goal'.

steps.title: Pose the Question
steps.act: You pose the question to audit — the repo, the roster, a decision, or a body of work.

steps.label: You confirm the angles to audit before the panel convenes.

steps.title: Convene the Panel
steps.act: Runs `guild/plan.py` — the Strategist's planning step as a runnable primitive.

steps.title: Converge & Grade
steps.act: The Strategist fires the panel, converges the takes, resolves contradictions, grades the result, and writes the ranked MD audit.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the verdict, the ranked findings, and the MD audit written — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Workflow Forge
workflows.tagline: Capture a finished run as a reusable star-map workflow.
workflows.when: Use this when a run is worth repeating — distil it into a named workflow, give it a sigil, and register it.

steps.title: Flag the Pattern
steps.act: You (or the Butler) flag that the just-finished run is worth saving as a star-map workflow.

steps.label: You approve capturing this run as a reusable workflow before it's drafted.

steps.title: Draft the Workflow
steps.act: The Strategist distils the run into a reusable workflow — name, trigger, members, weapons, and steps.

steps.title: Forge the Sigil
steps.act: The Designer forges the workflow's Fallen Sword sigil (runs Art Forge).

steps.title: Write to the Star Map
steps.act: The Quartermaster writes the new workflow into workflows.json, adds its art prompt, and rebuilds the dashboard.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the workflow registered, its sigil, and where it sits on the star map — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

trigger_phrases: save this run as a workflow

workflows.name: Relationship Intel
workflows.tagline: Turn email traffic into living relationship intelligence — win and keep clients.
workflows.when: Use this to profile contacts from Gmail, log mistakes to avoid, and recommend retention tasks. Resumable and checkpoint-driven; runs as a multi-agent harvest→profile→strategise pipeline.

steps.title: Call the Sweep
steps.act: You ask the guild to sweep your email and update the relationship intelligence.

steps.label: You confirm the window and the commercial scope before the sweep begins.

steps.title: Harvest the Threads
steps.act: The Herald sweeps Gmail (received and sent) for the window, returning a structured digest per contact — threads, response latency, tone, and commercial signals — saved as a dated harvest digest.

steps.title: Profile the Contacts
steps.act: The Herald enriches each contact profile — character, how to deal with them from the firm's angle, and mistakes to avoid — without overwriting their live items.

steps.title: Flag Risk & Opportunity
steps.act: The Strategist logs the mistakes to avoid, updates the recommended-tasks list, and flags at-risk relationships and opportunities to protect the firm's commercial interest.

steps.label: The Butler reports the contacts profiled, the mistakes logged, and the prioritised retention tasks — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Law Harvest
workflows.tagline: Ingest law PDFs into a clean, verified source-law library — the feed for the codex.
workflows.when: Use this to bring real law PDFs into the library — identify, rename, extract verbatim, verify, and index. Feeds Legal Codex (which then translates and publishes).

steps.title: Drop the Laws
steps.act: You add law PDFs to the Stock Laws folder, or ask the guild to process what's queued.

steps.label: You confirm the source folder and scope before the harvest runs.

steps.title: Scan & Queue the Stock
steps.act: The Architect scans the Stock Laws folder, identifies and renames each misnamed law PDF, resolves conflicts, and queues an extraction task.

steps.title: Extract Verbatim
steps.act: The Translator extracts the queued law verbatim — Arabic canonical, page-ranges intact — into Source Laws.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the extraction is faithful and complete — verbatim, no gaps — before it enters the Source Laws library.

steps.title: Index the Harvest
steps.act: The Translator updates the Source Laws index and writes the dated harvest digest.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs `guild/conformance.py` — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the laws harvested, verified, and indexed — and what's ready for the codex — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Oracle's Scrying
workflows.tagline: Scry the open web for a verified, cited answer to any question.
workflows.when: Use this when a question needs an answer from the live web — general knowledge, current facts, anything outside the repo or the guild. Triggered when you ask a question with 'search the web' in it. The Strategist fans out searches, cross-checks the strongest sources, and returns a cited answer; no code is written.

steps.title: Pose the Question
steps.act: You ask a question that needs an answer from the live web, with 'search the web' in it.

steps.label: You confirm the question and its scope before the scrying begins.

steps.title: Scry & Verify the Web
steps.act: The Strategist, driving the thinker weapon (web tools stay with the thinker — doers cannot run them), fans out web searches, fetches the strongest sources, adversarially cross-checks the claims, and synthesizes a cited answer.

steps.label: The Butler reports the answer and its sources — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

workflows.name: Inquiry / Recon
workflows.tagline: Answer a read-only question about the repo or guild — cited to file:line.
workflows.when: Use this for a quick read-only question about the code, the repo, or the guild itself — 'where is X', 'what calls Y', 'how does Z work', 'find all uses of W', 'which file holds …'. The routed member scouts with read-only tools (grep/glob/read), verifies findings against source, and returns a file:line-cited answer. No code is written, nothing is mutated. This is the lightweight lane so trivial lookups have a home under the workflow-gate hard rule, without the weight of a Strategic Audit.

steps.title: Pose the Question
steps.act: You ask a read-only question about the code, the repo, or the guild.

steps.title: Scout & Answer
steps.act: The routed member scouts read-only — grep/glob/read across the repo (handing bulk reads/summaries to a doer weapon) — verifies every claim against source, and returns a concise answer cited to file:line. Writes nothing.

steps.label: The Butler reports the answer and its file:line sources back to you in plain English, and flags whether the run is worth saving as a reusable workflow.

workflows.name: Local Session
workflows.tagline: Spawn an isolated Claude subagent to scout or work, then verify what it brings back.
workflows.when: Use this when the task should run in a fresh, isolated local session — 'start a new local session to investigate X', 'spawn a subagent', 'delegate this to a local Claude'. The routed member scopes the brief, spawns a CLAUDE session-doer via the Agent tool (a spawned session must be Claude — MiniMax doers run via summon.py and have NO tool access, so they cannot BE a session), gives it a disjoint scoped task, then verifies its findings against source before reporting. Pick the cheapest Claude doer the member carries: haiku where held (the-developer, the-translator, the-quartermaster), else sonnet.

steps.title: Pose the Session Task
steps.act: You ask for work to run in a fresh, isolated local session — an investigation or a scoped job.

steps.title: Scope & Spawn
steps.act: The routed member scopes a disjoint brief and spawns a CLAUDE session-doer via the Agent tool — haiku if the member carries it (dev/translator/quartermaster), else sonnet; NEVER MiniMax, whose summon.py doers have no tool access and cannot be a session. The subagent runs its scoped task in isolation and returns structured findings.

steps.title: Review & Synthesize
steps.act: The routed member verifies the subagent's findings against source — nothing trusted unverified — and synthesizes the answer.

steps.title: Confirm Conformance
steps.act: Runs guild/conformance.py if anything was mutated; for a read-only scout, confirms the session touched nothing and closes clean.

steps.label: The Butler reports the verified findings and their sources back to you in plain English, and flags whether the run is worth saving as a reusable workflow.

workflows.name: Conversation
workflows.tagline: Plain talk — a greeting, an ack, or a meta-question with no specialist lane.
workflows.when: The catch-all lane for any turn that fits no other workflow: greetings, acknowledgements, thanks, clarifications, and meta-questions about the guild itself. It exists so EVERY turn — even one that fires no tools and writes nothing — can declare a workflow under the turn-end banner gate. The Butler answers directly in plain English; nothing is mutated. If a turn turns out to need real work, route it to the proper specialist lane instead of staying here.

steps.title: Say the Word
steps.act: You greet, acknowledge, or ask a meta-question that fits no specialist lane.

steps.label: The Butler's plain-English reply IS the report back to you; he flags if the turn should have been a real workflow instead.

workflows.name: Harness Calibration
workflows.tagline: Fix, tune, and verify the harness itself.
workflows.when: Instrumentation broken, efficiency_report.py blind, hooks misfiring, skills need upgrade after audit, or LITE/FULL tier policy needs recalibration.

steps.title: Trigger calibration
steps.act: You name the broken instrumentation, misfiring hook, bad efficiency report, or skill gap that triggered this run.

steps.label: Restate brief (harness work, not feature work) and halt for explicit go.

steps.title: Audit harness
steps.act: Trace every hook, script, and measurement point. Find gaps where harness fails to capture, route, or report correctly. Produce concrete patch list.

steps.title: Patch hooks and scripts
steps.act: Apply patch list: fix broken hooks, correct script logic, repair measurement code, adjust LITE/FULL gating. Scoped to harness layer only. Verify data flows after patching.

steps.title: Upgrade affected skills
steps.act: Update skills surfaced by audit: remove stale content, add instructions for newly instrumented capabilities, bump version tags.

steps.title: Conformity close
steps.act: Re-run conformity_check.py. Verify no orphan hooks or dead scripts. Confirm all actors in new/changed workflows exist in guild-data.json.

steps.label: The Butler reports patch list applied, skills upgraded, conformity green — and flags any remaining gaps for a follow-up Harness Calibration.

workflows.name: Reflective Cycle
workflows.tagline: Turn a finished task into a durable guild upgrade.
workflows.when: Run after ANY non-trivial task — anything that touched code, doctrine, a workflow, or hit a retry/correction. Converts the run into a committed doctrine diff instead of throwing the lesson away. The closing half of every serious mission.

steps.title: Finish the Work
steps.act: A non-trivial task has just completed (code, doctrine, workflow, or a run with retries/corrections).

steps.title: Run the 6-Field Cycle
steps.act: Invokes guild-reflection (CYCLE mode): description (what ACTUALLY happened) → signals (friction) → evaluation (vs intent) → analysis (root cause, controllable vs not, trace to the SEED) → conclusion (one rule) → action_plan (a concrete diff naming a file). Delegate log/description drafting to a MiniMax doer; keep root-cause judgment in the thinker.

steps.title: Commit or File the Diff
steps.act: If the action_plan is small + safe, apply the diff to the named skill / CLAUDE.md / member trait now. If large or risky, file it to guild/journal backlog and surface to the Guild Master. Persist the record to guild/journal/cycle-<stamp>.json.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was learned and what changed. HARD RULE: a non-trivial 'done' with an empty action_plan is rejected — valid closes are an applied diff, a filed backlog item, or an explicit 'no change warranted — run was clean'.

workflows.name: Guild Self-Audit
workflows.tagline: Cadence sweep that weeds dead skills and retires bad ideas.
workflows.when: Run on a cadence (weekly or every N sessions) to keep the guild from rotting. Weeds skills that no longer earn their place, retires blocking assumptions with falsifying evidence, rebalances member load, and writes findings to the persistent journal. The recurring backbone of guild self-improvement.

steps.title: Call the Audit
steps.act: You (or a scheduled routine) trigger the cadence self-audit.

steps.title: Load Behavior
steps.act: Reads the last N guild/journal cycle records + audit reports, plus usage-log.jsonl and data/turn-cost.jsonl for real behavior. Self-knowledge is evidence-based, never introspective (Shoemaker).

steps.title: Weed, Retire, Rebalance, Interrogate
steps.act: Invokes guild-reflection (AUDIT mode): (1) weed skills with no productive retrieval in N cycles; (2) retire/replace held assumptions with evidence-for/falsifier/bad-idea-defended, appending to retired-ideas.md; (3) rebalance per-member load vs declared intent; (4) answer the fixed self-interrogation gut-honestly. Delegate scans/tallies to a MiniMax doer.

steps.title: Apply or Surface
steps.act: High-confidence + low-blast-radius diffs applied now; anything touching security/RLS/destructive scope is surfaced, never auto-applied. Writes guild/journal/audit-<stamp>.md and updates memory if a finding earns a durable file.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs guild/conformance.py — wraps conformity_check.py, writes a signoff markdown.

steps.label: The Butler reports the sweep outcome — skills weeded, ideas retired, imbalances flagged, diffs applied vs surfaced — in plain English.

workflows.name: App Packaging
workflows.tagline: Wrap a working surface into a real installable app.
workflows.when: Use when packaging an existing web/dashboard surface into a distributable desktop (or native) app — Tauri/Electron shell, a bundled server sidecar, icon, and a reproducible build. Not for feature work on the surface itself (that is Standard Mission / Architecture Build).

steps.title: Request the Package
steps.act: You name the surface to package and the target shell (e.g. Tauri desktop app with a server sidecar).

steps.label: You approve the packaging brief — toolchain installs and a new app shell are about to be created.

steps.title: Provision the Toolchain
steps.act: Verify and install the build prerequisites (Node, Rust/cargo, Tauri CLI, platform SDK). Records what was installed.

steps.title: Scaffold the Shell
steps.act: Create the app shell (src-tauri/ config, window, icon) pointing at the existing surface — no change to the surface's own code.

steps.title: Wire the Sidecar
steps.act: Bundle the server (serve.cjs + a Node runtime) as a sidecar the app spawns on launch and stops on quit, so the app is self-contained.

steps.title: Build and Smoke-Test
steps.act: Produce the app bundle, launch it, and confirm the surface loads and edits write through the sidecar.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the build is reproducible and the app is self-contained.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Run the conformity sweep, log the packaging, and confirm the repo still agrees with itself.

steps.label: The Butler reports back in plain English: the app is built, where it is, and how to launch it.

trigger_phrases: build it into a tauri app

workflows.name: Guild Recruitment
workflows.tagline: Scaffold a new guild member behind a gate — mandate, 3+ skills, a weapon, and a conformity pass.
workflows.when: Use when the guild needs a NEW member (a new specialist role) — not a new weapon (that is Arsenal Forge) or a new skill (that is Skill Forge).

steps.title: Name the Role
steps.act: You name the role the guild lacks and the work it would own.

steps.label: You approve the role is real and worth a seat before it is scaffolded. Recruitment gate: the member must own 3 or more unique skills, at least one provisioned weapon, and pass conformity.

steps.title: Design the Member
steps.act: The Strategist defines the member's mandate, the gap it fills, its three-or-more unique skills, and the seat/weapon it wields — and verifies it does not duplicate an existing member.

steps.title: Forge the Member Art
steps.act: The Designer commissions the member portrait via MiniMax and reviews it (runs Art Forge).

steps.title: Scaffold the Member
steps.act: The Quartermaster writes the member dossier under star-alliance-members/, the members-meta entry, the domain assignment, and the Skill Drills rows, then rebuilds the dashboard.

steps.title: Confirm Guild Conformance
steps.act: Runs guild/conformance.py — wraps conformity_check.py and writes a signoff markdown; recruitment fails the gate if the member lacks 3 skills or a weapon.

steps.label: The Butler reports the member recruited — its mandate, skills, weapon, and the conformity result — back to you in plain English, and flags whether this run could be saved as a reusable star-map workflow.

trigger_phrases: the guild needs a new specialist
